title: Iran War Live Updates: Israel Strikes Southern Lebanon After Pulling Back From Threat to Beirut
summary: Under pressure from President Trump, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel held off from attacking Beirut. But he vowed to continue Israel’s campaign against Hezbollah, which could threaten peace talks with Iran.

title: Housing Official Who Attacked Democrats Invokes a Disputed Family Legacy
summary: The Trump nominee Bill Pulte has called out a Fed governor and New York’s attorney general for issues with their mortgage documents. Some leaders of the family’s home-building business bristle at how he has enhanced his reputation.

title: Extreme Water Shortages in the West, and Hegseth’s Pentagon Purge
summary: Plus, the rocky, unpredictable race for California governor.

title: Russia Launches Deadly Strikes on Kyiv After Threatening Ukraine for a Week
summary: Moscow’s repeated warnings of a major strike, combined with the delay before it happened, seemed intended to inflict a psychological toll on the Ukrainian capital.

title: Russia Is Showing Signs of Weakness in Ukraine. So It Hits Harder.
summary: The war has not been going the Kremlin’s way, with battleground losses and mounting casualties. With fiercer strikes, Moscow hopes to gain a better position for negotiations.

title: What to Watch in Tuesday’s Primary Elections in Iowa, Montana and Beyond
summary: Democrats have a competitive Senate primary race in Iowa, where the party is hoping to make gains, while a smokejumper is running in a contested House primary in Montana.

title: Group Pledges $2 Million to Fight AIPAC in House Races
summary: A super PAC created to counter spending from powerful pro-Israel groups will try to help Democrats critical of Israel’s wars and military actions.

title: Iowa Democrats Seek an Outsider in an Anti-Establishment Year
summary: Tuesday’s Senate primary features two candidates with compelling personal stories. Both have stressed their independence.

title: Hospitals See Diseases Resurge as Vaccinations Decline
summary: Doctors nationwide are encountering more children with whooping cough, bacterial infections and other serious illnesses, as well as more adults refusing tetanus shots.

title: Obama Center’s Two Sides: A Lovely Park and a Forbidding Tower
summary: In Chicago, the $850 million Obama Presidential Center aims to remake a neighborhood with a 19.3-acre community hub and a brooding 225-foot museum.

title: Trump Megadonor Gave $5.5 Million Estate to Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s Anti-Vaccine Group
summary: A Mellon heir transferred the adjacent Connecticut properties, covering about 300 acres, at no cost to Children’s Health Defense last year.

title: What Ireland and Germany Can Teach Us About Birthright Citizenship
summary: President Trump has argued that the United States is “stupid” for granting citizenship at birth. Most countries don’t do so, but that can create problems.

title: How Hezbollah Drones Changed Israel’s Strategy in Lebanon
summary: An Israeli campaign that started with high hopes has devolved into a kind of impasse, with Hezbollah looking more capable than it did when the war began.

title: A Whole Generation Seems to Be Using ‘POV’ Wrong
summary: Gen Z might be using the abbreviation for “point of view” incorrectly. But linguists think it’s exciting.

title: My Two Sleepless Months With the Eight Sleep Pod
summary: The luxury gadget promises to hack sleep — and relationships. But can anything?

title: Mamdani Is Under Pressure to Act Amid Slowing Job Growth in New York
summary: As New York City faces slowing job growth and broader economic concerns, Mayor Zohran Mamdani has not offered a detailed plan to create jobs.

title: Mamdani Advances 34th St. Bus Lane Plan That U.S. Had Blocked
summary: The city is moving forward with a redesign of 34th Street in Manhattan that the White House halted last year, in hopes of significantly improving bus speeds.

title: Why America Is Its Own Biggest Geopolitical Risk
summary: The political risk analyst Ian Bremmer argues that the greatest driver of risk in the world right now is President Trump.

title: Inside the Ebola Outbreak
summary: We discuss the epidemic in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

title: Late Night Updates America on the Lineup for Its 250th Birthday Bash
summary: “Instead of music, the entertainment will be an 80-year-old man yelling about windmills,” Jimmy Kimmel said after President Trump said he would headline a summer concert series.

title: U.S. Ebola Unit Plans in Kenya, Subject of Protests, Suffers New Setback From Court Ruling
summary: The court further delayed the Trump administration’s proposed quarantine unit for Americans exposed to the virus. The plan has sparked angry protests in Kenya.
